name: subfinder
purpose: Passive subdomain enumeration via 40+ public sources including CT logs, passive DNS, and security APIs
author: Bhomesh Koli (Ice3man543)
why_notable: Bhomesh Koli, an Indian bug bounty hunter, wrote subfinder in Go around 2018 to fix the core problems of Python-based predecessors (sublist3r, knock) — serial HTTP requests, constant rate-limiting, no structured output. Go goroutines let subfinder query 50+ passive DNS APIs simultaneously at almost zero overhead, producing a deduplicated list with zero packets sent to the target. It became the flagship tool of ProjectDiscovery, which grew into a full security tooling company (Series A: $25M in 2024). The Chaos dataset — a continuously updated database of subdomains for every major bug bounty program — is one of subfinder's most valuable sources and a ProjectDiscovery contribution to the community.
